evidence: Our original product was incredible for what it was, but it's kind of like a widget. It does its job really, really well, but it's not particularly innovative. And we sort of tapped out the amount of value that we can give our customers.
Multi-product, AI-powered relationship management. Core ICP: People who sell their time. These customers don't have CRMs.

High-status individuals need to manufacture the appearance of busyness, while most productivity tools assume users want maximum efficiency/availability.

evidence: Platform adoption typically comes through direct utility or ecosystem effects. V proposes a third vector: skill acquisition as a side effect of product use.

V is building AI systems to generate content that can beat AI detection systems. This creates a recursive loop: as AI-generated content improves, detection must improve.
Building a voice engine that analyzes V's LinkedIn posts and comments. Goal: Generate AI drafts that pass Pangram (AI detection tool).
V describes being 'possessed by the holy spirit' of vibe coding - working through the night in flow state, building complete products in single prompts.
Traditional startup wisdom emphasizes sustainable pace, process, repeatability. Vibe coding is the opposite: inspiration-driven, sporadic, high-intensity bursts.
Anthropic 'pulled the rug' on Claude Code access, immediately affecting V's workflow despite paying $100/month for max tier.
Every AI-native workflow is built on platforms that can change terms overnight. V's entire productivity infrastructure depends on decisions made by companies with no obligation to him.
Built an interview feedback tool using Zo's new Stripe integration in a single prompt. Amanda tested it and found the feedback 'very, very decent, very constructive'.
Release 3 podcast episodes as a batch rather than weekly drops. Weekly drops suggest ongoing series commitment. Batch release creates 'splash' and more eyeballs.
